channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: U.S. offers $10 million for hackers targeting WhatsApp, Signal users
item.description: The U.S. Department of State is offering up to $10 million for information that helps identify or locate members of the UNC5792 and UNC4221 hacker groups, which are linked to Russia's intelligence and...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Kernel Flaws, AI Malware Tricks, Turla Backdoor, Infostealers and More
item.description:
This week was a reminder that attackers do not always need big tricks. One small mistake, one old access path, one missed patch, and suddenly the door is open.
The noise is not all noise, either. For...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Agentic AI Has an Identity Problem and Attackers Know It
item.description: AI agents can access data, trigger workflows, and take action across enterprise systems. Token Security explains why governing these privileged identities is becoming essential for enterprise security...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical SimpleHelp flaw exploited to deploy new stealer malware
item.description: Hackers are exploiting a recently disclosed critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-48558) in SimpleHelp to deploy Djinn Stealer, a previously undocumented cross-platform information stealer targeting Window...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers now exploit critical Oracle E-Business flaw in attacks
item.description: Attackers have begun exploiting a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-46817) in the Oracle E-Business Suite (EBS) financial application, according to threat intelligence company Defused. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Webinar: Why business email compromise attacks keep succeeding
item.description: Business email compromise attacks increasingly rely on convincing impersonation rather than malware, making them harder for employees and traditional email defenses to detect. This webinar explores ho...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 236,000 DCloud Uni-App Sites Used in Crypto Scams, Phishing, and Wallet Drainers
item.description: New findings unearthed by Infoblox show that more than 236,000 websites are using investment scam templates built using a legitimate Chinese open-source, cross-platform application development framewo...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Why Post-Quantum Cryptography Starts With Credentials
item.description: Today’s encrypted data, such as credentials, may no longer remain confidential in the future because the public-key cryptography protecting it will soon be broken by quantum computers. Although no mac...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Gamaredon Expands Ukraine Attacks with New Malware and Cloud Service Abuse
item.description:
A Russian advanced persistent threat (APT) group has continued to evolve and expand its malware arsenal as part of its ongoing cyber onslaught against Ukraine throughout 2025.
Slovakian cybersecurity...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: US seizes hundreds of FIFA World Cup illegal streaming domains
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department's Criminal Division has seized nearly 400 web domains used for illegally streaming matches at the FIFA World Cup. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Removes 119 Edge Extensions That Hid Malware in Images and Fonts
item.description: Microsoft has shut down a long-running malicious extension operation on the Edge Add-ons store that hid its payloads inside ordinary image and font files, then woke up days after install to steal cred...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Public PoC Released for Critical libssh2 CVE-2026-55200 Client-Side SSH Flaw
item.description: A public proof-of-concept is now out for CVE-2026-55200, a critical flaw in libssh2 that lets a malicious or compromised SSH server trigger memory corruption on a connecting client, with possible code...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hijacked npm and Go Packages Use VS Code Tasks to Deploy Python Infostealer
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered two hijacked npm packages and a cluster of Go packages that are designed to deploy a Python-based information stealer on compromised Windows, Linux, and macOS ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Data breach exposes up to 14.2 million email logins at six ISPs
item.description: Japanese telecommunications operator KDDI Corporation disclosed a data breach where threat actors gained access to one of its email systems used by five other internet service providers (ISPs) in the ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ukraine Says Russian Intelligence Used Fake Support Texts to Steal Messaging Credentials
item.description: The Security Service of Ukraine (SSU) said it, together with the U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), uncovered a long-running campaign orchestrated by Russian intelligence services to break in...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Clean GitHub repo tricks AI coding agents into running malware
item.description: An agentic coding tool tasked with cloning and setting up a seemingly benign GitHub repository could execute a malicious payload that remains invisible to security scanners, AI agents, and human revie...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: OpenAI Previews GPT-5.6 Sol With Restricted Access and Stronger Cyber Safeguards
item.description:
OpenAI on Friday released three versions of GPT-5.6, called Sol, Terra, and Luna, as a limited preview to a small number of companies as part of an ongoing engagement with the U.S. government.
While ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Third-Party Breaches Teach Education Sector a Costly Lesson in Vendor Risk
item.description: Rising threats from third-party actors are forcing institutions to play defense to protect student data from ransomware and other attacks....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: FBI: Russian hackers now target Signal backup recovery keys
item.description: The FBI and CISA are warning that a phishing campaign targeting Signal users tied to Russian intelligence services has evolved to steal Signal Backup Recovery Keys, allowing attackers to access victim...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA sets urgent deadline to fix Cisco flaw exploited in attacks
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is giving federal agencies until Sunday to patch a vulnerability in Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server that is being actively ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FBI Warns Russian Intelligence Hackers Target Signal Backup Recovery Keys
item.description: The FBI and CISA have updated their March warning about Russian intelligence phishing Signal accounts, and the operators have added a step: they now coax targets into handing over their Signal Backup ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: AI Decline? Confidence in Autonomous Penetration Testing Falls
item.description: Companies are still experimenting with automated AI systems to find security weaknesses, but fewer are relying on the technology....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New SharkLoader Malware Deploys Cobalt Strike in StrikeShark Cyberattacks
item.description: A newly discovered cyber attack campaign has been observed delivering a previously undocumented malware family called SharkLoader that acts as a loader for deploying Cobalt Strike Beacon on compromise...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Polymarket customers lose $3 million in supply-chain attack
item.description: Polymarket says it will fully reimburse customers who lost an estimated $3 million after hackers injected a malicious script into the platform's frontend following a breach at a third-party vendor. [....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cybersecurity firms targeted by fraudulent OpenAI organization invites
item.description: Threat actors are creating OpenAI tenants that impersonate legitimate companies and inviting employees to join them, in what appears to be a ploy to trick targets into submitting sensitive company inf...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
item.description: Cisco joins a growing list of security platform providers that are betting that securing the agentic workforce means turning identity into the primary control plane....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New Initiative Tackles Security for End-of-Life Open Source Software
item.description: The Open Source Sustainability Initiative's goal is to help enterprises manage and secure aging open source projects while maintaining regulatory compliance....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Chinese-Speaking APT Deploys New TinyRCT Backdoor in Southeast Asia Campaign
item.description: A Chinese-speaking advanced persistent threat (APT) actor has been linked to a new custom backdoor called TinyRCT as part of cyber attacks aimed at government entities and critical infrastructure in S...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: AI Won't Wipe-Out Entry-Level Cybersecurity Jobs
item.description: Instead of eliminating jobs for early-career cyber pros, AI is creating new opportunities for candidates with strong human decision-making skills....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Your First GRC Agent: A Red Teamer's Walkthrough
item.description: AI won't replace GRC analysts, but it can eliminate much of the repetitive work they do. Anecdotes walks through building an agent that continuously monitors controls, identifies evidence gaps, and op...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
